content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 28, 1995

George Albert, CPA
731 77th Street
Oakland, CA 94621-2588

Re: Your Request For Advice
Our File No. A-95-036

Dear Mr. Albert:

We write in response to your request for advice regarding the
use of surplus campaign funds by the Holmdahl Re-Election Campaign
Committee under the applicable provisions of the Political Reform
Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

May a candidate committee use its surplus campaign funds to
pay salaries for staff members and make donations to charitable,
civic, and educational tax-exempt organizations?

CONCLUSION

Yes. Surplus campaign funds may be allocated to pay final
staff expenses and to make charitable donations.

FACTS

The Holmdahl Re-Election Campaign Committee wishes to make a
final distribution of surplus funds. The committee desires to
make contributions to the following tax-exempt organizations:

American Heart Association
Boy Scouts of America, National Organization
Boy Scouts of America, San Francisco-Bay Area Council
Chabot College
Educational Guidance Institute
Focus on the Family
Greater Bay Area Make-A-Wish Foundation
Project Reality

_______________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. A-95-036
Page 2


San Leandro Boys' and Girls' Club
The Salvation Army

     Additionally the committee must make a final payment to Sandy
Libbrecht, a consultant who worked for the committee during its
closure.

                              ANALYSIS

     Section 89519 provides that upon leaving any elected office,
or at the end of the postelection reporting period following the
defeat of a candidate for elective office, campaign funds raised
after January 1, 1989, under the control of the former candidate
or elected officer, are considered surplus.  Surplus campaign
funds may be used for, among other things, the payment of any
outstanding campaign debts, donations to charitable, educational,
civic, religious, or similar tax-exempt, nonprofit organization,
and for the payment of professional services reasonably required
by the committee to assist in the performance of its
administrative functions.  (Section 89519.)  It should be noted
that if surplus campaign funds are used to make donations, no part
of the proceeds may have a material financial effect on the former
candidate or elected officer or any member of his or her immediate
family, or his or her campaign treasurer.

     It is apparent from the documentation you have submitted with
your request for advice that each of the organizations you have
identified qualify under Section 89519 as charitable, educational,
civic, religious, or similar tax-exempt, nonprofit organizations.
Accordingly, as long as no part of the donated funds will
materially financially affect the candidate or the candidate's
immediate family, or the campaign treasurer, the donations will be
acceptable uses of surplus campaign funds.  Additionally, the
supplement to your request, dated February 8, 1995, states that
the payment to Ms. Libbrecht is for administrative work closing
out the committee.  Accordingly, the payment to Ms. Libbrecht is
an acceptable use of surplus campaign funds.

     We trust that the advice contained herein adequately answers
the questions you have raised.  Should you have any additional
questions, please contact the undersigned at (916) 322-5901.


                              Sincerely,

                              Steven G. Churchwell
                              General Counsel
                              [signature]
                              By:  Daniel E. Muallem
                                   Counsel, Legal Division

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May a candidate committee use its surplus campaign funds to
pay salaries for staff members and make donations to charitable,
civic,
and educational tax—exempt organizations?

CONCLUSION: Yes.
Surplus campaign funds may be allocated to pay final
staff expenses and to make charitable donations.
